items.title: Feds seek lower prison term for $100 million New Jersey deli fraudster — but some reasons why are hidden
items.description: James Patten is the third person who will be sentenced in the stock manipulation scheme related to Hometown International, a company that owned just one deli.

items.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
items.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

items.title: First known congressional SpaceX stock buys surface after record IPO
items.description: The SpaceX stock purchases come as Elon Musk’s company deepens its role in federal contracting and remains closely tied to President Donald Trump’s Washington.

items.title: American Express and Chase move luxury lounge wars beyond the airport
items.description: Credit card companies are increasingly offering access to lounges and perks at festivals and sporting events, often exclusively for premium cardholders.

items.title: Acting DNI Pulte fires dozens of intelligence officials: MS Now
items.description: President Donald Trump's controversial pick to lead the office of the Director of National Intelligence has begun firing intel officials, MS NOW reported.

items.title: The hunt for AI's next winners defined the stock market's holiday-shortened week
items.description: Wall Street kicked off the third quarter with mixed results after a blockbuster first half for stocks.

items.title: Don't throw away your shot to speak to an AI Alexander Hamilton at Boston's new finance museum
items.description: Nearly a decade after leaving its former home, the Museum of American Finance is opening its new doors to the public.

items.title: AI is outpacing the rules, Europe’s top bankers and regulators warn
items.description: Europe's top bankers and financial regulators are grappling with how to better regulate AI risks.

items.title: Ford achieves quality milestone, as CEO targets flawless new vehicle launches
items.description: Ford CEO Jim Farley told CNBC that the automaker has learned from its past quality and recall issues, which have hurt its earnings and stained its reputation.

items.title: Trump’s Freedom 250 draws corporate sponsors with business before his administration
items.description: Companies with federal interests — including Boeing, Lockheed Martin and Oracle — are sponsoring a Trump-aligned effort celebrating America’s 250th birthday.

items.title: As ACA enrollment falls by millions, Trump administration and policy gurus disagree on why
items.description: Affordable Care Act marketplace enrollment has declined by 3 million people. The Trump administration points to fraud controls. Policy experts point to cost.

items.title: Manhattan luxury real estate sales hold firm despite fears of a 'Mamdani effect'
items.description: A month after the passage of a tax on second homes in New York City, brokers and analysts said sales of luxury real estate remain strong.

items.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
items.description: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

items.title: Gold prices set for first weekly rise in a month as investors scale back Fed rate hike bets
items.description: The metal was boosted as investors scaled back their bets on a looming rate hike.

items.title: Oman walks a diplomatic tightrope over Strait of Hormuz fees, creating a ‘blind spot’ for markets
items.description: Oman is using strategic ambiguity as talks over possible Strait of Hormuz fees raise legal, diplomatic and oil market concerns.

items.title: Safe havens aren't behaving like they used to. Here's what's changed
items.description: Traditional safe-haven assets — U.S. Treasurys, the Japanese yen and gold — have struggled to provide protection during this year’s market volatility.

items.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
items.description: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

items.title: Venezuela quake toll climbs to 2,595 as damage estimates mount after strongest tremor in a century
items.description: Death toll from twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela jumped to 2,595, as rescue operations continued after the country's strongest tremors in over a century.

items.title: Nvidia offers start-up customers chance to swap compute power for revenue share
items.description: Nvidia says it will give start-ups the chance to swap access to compute power for a slice of future profits under a new program aimed at AI-focused firms.

items.title: 5 takeaways from President Donald Trump's interview with CNBC
items.description: The president defended his family's business and said he feels bad that the presidency creates a conflict of interest in any of his children's investments.
